reasoning: The pro forma OIBOC statements in Item 19 are based on 11,332 independent franchisee traditional restaurants whose royalties were 4% of Gross Sales during the covered period. However, per Item 6 Footnote 2, new restaurants opened after January 1, 2024 pay a 5% royalty. This 100 basis point difference means the pro forma overstates OIBOC for new franchisees by approximately 1% of gross sales. At the median franchised sales of $3,797,000, this represents approximately $37,970 per year in additional costs not reflected in the pro forma. The FDD explicitly notes the 4% royalty in the pro forma footnotes but does not compute the dollar impact of the difference for prospective new franchisees.
quote_fragments: The royalties payable by these franchised restaurants during the covered period were 4% of Gross Sales
The royalty of 5% of Gross Sales applies on and after January 1, 2024, in the following scenarios: (i) a franchisee opens a new restaurant
